general.q: What is included in your kitchen installation service?
general.a: Our standard installation service covers the full assembly and fitting of all kitchen carcasses, doors, drawers, plinths, cornices, and cornice lighting pelmet where specified. We also connect the sink and tap to existing supplies, fit integrated appliances, and complete all scribing and infill panels for a neat, professional finish. Worktop templating and fitting is included where we are supplying the worktop; if you are supplying your own we will still fit it as part of the service.

general.q: What is a dry fit and why do you recommend it?
general.a: A dry fit is the process of assembling and positioning all cabinets and panels in the kitchen space without final fixing, allowing us to check that every unit is level, every door aligns correctly, and the overall layout works perfectly before anything is permanently secured. It is an essential quality step that helps us identify any issues with the room's dimensions or the kitchen's components before they become more difficult to resolve. We always carry out a full dry fit on every installation as standard.

general.q: How long does a kitchen installation take?
general.a: Installation times vary depending on the size of the kitchen and the complexity of the project, but as a general guide a small kitchen of 8–12 units typically takes 3–4 days, a medium kitchen of 13–20 units takes 4–6 days, and a large kitchen of 21 or more units takes 6–10 days. These timescales include fitting all cabinets, doors, worktops, and integrated appliances but do not include any preparatory plastering, tiling, or electrical work carried out by other trades. We will give you a specific timeline estimate during your pre-installation survey.

general.q: Do I need to arrange my own plumber and electrician?
general.a: Yes — our installation service focuses on the cabinetry, worktops, and fitting of integrated appliances, and we work alongside your independently appointed plumber and electrician. We strongly recommend that you book your plumber and electrician before confirming your installation date, as their work typically needs to take place at specific stages during the installation. We are happy to advise on the best sequence of works and can recommend trusted local tradespeople if you need introductions.

We cover Coventry and the wider Warwickshire area, including Rugby, Nuneaton, Bedworth, Leamington Spa, Kenilworth, Warwick, Stratford-upon-Avon, and all surrounding villages. We also regularly work in parts of the West Midlands including Solihull, Meriden, and Balsall Common, and across the border into Leicestershire including Hinckley. If you are unsure whether we cover your area, please get in touch and we will confirm.

general.q: Which kitchen retailers and brands do you work with?
general.a: We fit kitchens from all major UK retailers and manufacturers, including Howdens, Wren, Magnet, Benchmarx, IKEA, B&Q, Wickes, DIY Kitchens, Kutchenhaus, Symphony, and Masterclass, as well as premium brands such as Neptune, deVOL, Smallbone, and Häcker. If your kitchen supplier is not listed here, please contact us — in most cases we will be happy to fit it. Our only requirement is that the product is a standard UK-format carcass, whether flat-pack or rigid-built.

Yes, we can supply and fit a range of worktops including solid oak, solid walnut, laminate, and compact laminate surfaces. For stone, quartz, and solid-surface worktops we work with trusted specialist fabricators who will template and fit the worktop as part of your overall installation project. If you have purchased your own worktop we are happy to fit it; please let us know the material and dimensions at the survey stage so we can plan accordingly.

general.q: What happens if something is damaged during installation?
general.a: We take every care to protect your home and your kitchen components throughout the installation, and we are fully insured for accidental damage to property. In the rare event that a cabinet door, panel, or worktop is damaged during fitting, we will report this to you immediately and work with your retailer or our own suppliers to arrange a prompt replacement. Any damage caused by our team will be rectified at no cost to you, and we will not consider the project complete until you are entirely satisfied with the finished result.

survey.q: What happens at the pre-installation survey?
survey.a: Our surveyor will visit your home to take detailed measurements of the kitchen space, assess the condition of the existing floor, walls, and services, and review your kitchen plan with you to identify any potential issues before installation day. We check things like ceiling height, wall straightness, existing pipework routes, and appliance positions to ensure the installation will proceed smoothly. The survey report forms the basis of our fixed installation quote.

survey.q: How much does the survey cost?
survey.a: Our pre-installation survey is priced at £195, which covers the site visit, detailed measurement, and a written installation quote. If you proceed with the installation, the survey fee is deducted in full from your final invoice. The survey is carried out by an experienced fitter rather than a salesperson, so you receive genuinely technical advice about your project.

survey.q: Do I need to have my kitchen fully designed before booking a survey?
survey.a: You do not need a finalised kitchen design to book a survey, but it is helpful to have at least a provisional layout from your chosen retailer so our surveyor can cross-reference it against the actual room dimensions. If significant discrepancies are found between the plan and the room, we will flag these to you and your retailer so they can be resolved before ordering. The earlier we are involved in the design process, the better the outcome for you.

survey.q: What should I prepare before the survey visit?
survey.a: Please ensure the kitchen space is accessible and that the surveyor can see all walls, including any that may be behind the existing kitchen if you are replacing an old one. It is also helpful to have a copy of your kitchen plan or order confirmation to hand, along with details of any appliances you intend to have fitted. If you have any specific concerns about the room — such as a sloping floor, an awkward corner, or a boiler that needs relocating — please mention these at the start of the survey.

survey.q: Will my existing kitchen need to be stripped out before installation?
survey.a: Yes, the existing kitchen must be fully stripped out before we can begin fitting the new one, and this stripping-out work is included in our standard installation service at no additional charge. If there is significant remedial work to the walls, floor, or ceiling revealed after strip-out — such as damaged plaster or rotten floorboards — this will need to be addressed by a plasterer or builder before we can proceed, and we will advise you of this during the survey where possible.

survey.q: Can you advise on the best layout for my kitchen?
survey.a: While our primary expertise is in installation rather than interior design, our surveyors have fitted hundreds of kitchens and are well placed to offer practical advice on layout efficiency, appliance positioning, and common pitfalls to avoid. We are happy to review your retailer's plan and suggest amendments where we think they would improve the finished result or simplify the installation. For a full design service, we recommend working with your chosen kitchen retailer's designer in conjunction with our technical input.

retailers.q: Can you fit a kitchen I have bought from any retailer?
retailers.a: Yes — we are genuinely retailer-agnostic and fit kitchens from all UK suppliers, whether you have bought from a high street showroom like Magnet or Wren, a trade supplier like Howdens or Benchmarx, an online retailer like DIY Kitchens, or a superstore like IKEA or B&Q. The only requirement is that the product is a standard UK-format carcass; we do not fit bespoke cabinetry constructed in non-standard sizes without a prior survey. Contact us if you are unsure.

retailers.q: Is there a difference in installation cost between retailers?
retailers.a: Installation costs are primarily driven by the number of units, the complexity of the layout, and the number of days required, rather than the retailer. However, flat-pack formats such as IKEA require additional on-site assembly time compared to pre-built rigid carcasses, which is reflected in a slightly higher day rate for flat-pack projects. We apply a transparent modifier to our base rates for flat-pack and particularly complex premium brands, and this will be clearly itemised in your quote.

retailers.q: Do you have a preferred retailer you recommend?
retailers.a: We do not have commercial relationships with any specific retailer and genuinely recommend the best option for each client based on their budget, style preferences, and priorities. For value, we often suggest Howdens or DIY Kitchens for their rigid-build quality at competitive prices. For premium projects, we frequently work with Masterclass, Symphony, and independent suppliers of German brands such as Häcker and Rotpunkt. We are happy to advise at the survey stage.

retailers.q: What do I need to arrange with my retailer before installation day?
retailers.a: You should confirm with your retailer that all items in your order have been picked and are ready for delivery well in advance of your installation date. We recommend requesting a complete packing list and checking it against your order confirmation to ensure nothing has been missed or substituted. Any missing or damaged items should be reported to the retailer immediately so that replacements can be sourced before installation day, as delays caused by missing components can push back the entire project timeline.

retailers.q: Can you help if my kitchen arrives with damaged or missing parts?
retailers.a: Yes — dealing with damaged or missing components is a routine part of the installation process, and our team is experienced in identifying issues quickly and liaising with retailers to resolve them. We always carry out a thorough check of all delivered components on the first day of installation and flag any problems to you and your retailer at the earliest opportunity. Where possible, we will work around minor issues and complete as much of the installation as we can while awaiting replacement parts.

retailers.q: Do you work with trade-only suppliers like Howdens and Benchmarx?
retailers.a: Yes — we hold active trade accounts with Howdens Joinery and Benchmarx Kitchens, which means we can order your kitchen directly on your behalf and collect it from the local depot. This trade route offers several advantages: there is no retail delivery to your home, components are checked at the depot before leaving, and any missing items can usually be sourced same-day from stock. If you would like us to supply through one of these channels, we will handle the ordering process and include the kitchen cost within your overall project quote.

costs.q: How much does kitchen installation cost in Coventry?
costs.a: Installation costs in Coventry and the surrounding Warwickshire area typically range from around £1,800–£2,800 for a small kitchen of 8–12 units, £2,800–£4,200 for a medium kitchen of 13–20 units, and £4,200–£7,500 for a large kitchen of 21 or more units. These figures represent labour and fitting only and do not include the cost of the kitchen itself, worktops, appliances, plumbing, or electrical work. We provide a fixed price quote following your survey so you will know the exact cost before committing.

costs.q: Is VAT included in your prices?
costs.a: All prices quoted on this website and in our survey quotations are inclusive of VAT at the current standard rate of 20%. We are a VAT-registered business and will provide a full VAT invoice upon completion of your installation. If you require a breakdown of the net and VAT amounts for your own records, please request this from our office.

costs.q: Do you charge for stripping out the old kitchen?
costs.a: Strip-out of the existing kitchen is included within our standard installation quote at no additional charge, covering the removal of all existing cabinets, plinths, cornices, and worktops. We will also disconnect the existing sink from the water supply before the plumber makes the permanent connections. Disposal of the stripped-out kitchen is included; if you wish to retain any elements for reuse or donation, please let us know before the installation begins.

costs.q: Are there any additional costs I should budget for?
costs.a: Beyond the kitchen itself and our installation fee, the most common additional costs are plumbing (typically £300–£600 for a straightforward kitchen), electrical work (£200–£500 for sockets, lighting, and appliance connections), and tiling (variable depending on area). If your walls or floor require remedial work after strip-out, there may be plastering or flooring costs to factor in. We will flag any issues we identify at the survey stage so you can budget accordingly.

Yes, we ask for a deposit of 30% of the total installation fee upon confirmation of your booking date, with the remaining 70% due on completion of the installation. The deposit secures your slot in our installation schedule and covers initial material costs. All payment terms are clearly set out in your written quote and booking confirmation.

costs.q: Can I spread the cost of installation?
costs.a: At present we do not offer in-house finance, but we accept all major debit and credit cards, bank transfer, and cheque. Many of our clients use personal loans or 0% purchase credit cards to spread the cost of the overall kitchen project, which can be a sensible approach when balancing kitchen, worktop, appliance, and installation costs. Please speak to your bank or a financial adviser for guidance on the most appropriate borrowing option for your circumstances.

process.q: What is the typical sequence of works for a kitchen installation?
process.a: The typical sequence begins with the pre-installation survey, followed by ordering the kitchen and booking the installation date. On the first day of installation we strip out the old kitchen and carry out a dry fit of all new carcasses; the plumber then first fixes the pipework. We then permanently fix and level all carcasses, fit the worktops (which may require a separate stone templating visit), and the plumber second-fixes the sink and tap. Finally we fit all doors, drawers, handles, plinths, cornices, and integrated appliances, and the electrician completes their connections before we carry out a final snagging check.

process.q: Will I be without a functioning kitchen during the installation?
process.a: Yes, during the installation period you will not have access to a fully functioning kitchen, and you should plan meals and access to water accordingly. Most installations take between 3 and 10 days depending on size; we recommend setting up a temporary kitchen area with a kettle, microwave, and access to cold water in another room. We work as efficiently as possible to minimise the disruption period, and we will give you a clear daily progress update so you always know where the project stands.

process.q: Do I need to be at home during the installation?
process.a: You do not need to be present throughout the entire installation, but we do ask that a responsible adult is available at the start of each day to grant access and at the end of each day for a progress review. It is also important that someone is available to make decisions about any unforeseen issues that arise during the fit — for example, if a wall turns out to be in a different position to the plan, we may need guidance on how to proceed. We will always call ahead if we need to discuss anything urgently.

process.q: What preparation do I need to do before installation day?
process.a: Before installation begins, please ensure the kitchen space is completely cleared of all personal items, food, and small appliances. The adjacent rooms that we will use as a working area should also be clear, and we ask that pets are kept away from the work area for safety reasons. If you have not already done so, please confirm your plumber and electrician bookings and share their contact details with us so we can co-ordinate the programme of works.

On the final day of installation, our lead fitter will carry out a full snagging walk-round with you, checking every door, drawer, hinge, and fitting for alignment, function, and finish. Any items that do not meet our standard will be adjusted or replaced on the spot where possible, or flagged for a follow-up visit if a replacement part is required. We ask you to sign off the completed installation only when you are fully satisfied, and we remain available to return for any warranty-related issues within the first twelve months.

process.q: What warranty do you provide on your installation?
process.a: We provide a 12-month workmanship warranty on all installation work, covering any defects arising from the fitting process such as carcasses becoming unlevel, hinges failing, or plinths coming loose. This warranty is in addition to any manufacturer's warranty provided by your kitchen supplier, which typically covers the product itself for a period of 5–25 years depending on the brand. Any warranty claim should be reported to us in writing, and we will arrange a remedial visit within 10 working days.
